title: verify-build findings buffer
description: Schema for the structured findings buffer written by /flow:verify-build Step 8. Consumed by /flow:ship Step 4a (FB-XXXX synthesis) and by the future HTML case-study renderer (PR R candidate). Shape is a superset of what each consumer needs so neither needs a schema migration.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking changes; never on additive changes.

branch.description: Git branch the verify pass ran against (`git branch --show-current`).

head_sha_short.description: Short SHA of HEAD at verify time (`git rev-parse --short HEAD`).

plugin_version.description: Version of the flow plugin that produced this buffer (read from plugin.json).

platform_hint.description: Project platform as resolved by bundled /run; informs which not-tested checklist to use. 'unknown' when /run autodetect was ambiguous.

verify_budget_calls_used.description: Tool-call count consumed by bundled /verify during Step 5. Compared against flow.config.json.verifyBudgetCalls.

verify_budget_overrun.description: True if budget cap fired and all unjudged verdicts were forced to Unknown.

spike_mode.description: True ONLY for an explicit spike declaration (invoked via /flow:ship-spike — trigger 1). NO LONGER set for a missing/Spec-walk-less plan; that is `no_plan_fallback`. Spike's reduced rigor (3-check rubric) requires the explicit declaration, never merely an absent plan.

no_plan_fallback.description: True when verify-build ran WITHOUT a governing plan because none was found (missing planPath — trigger 2 — or no **Spec-walk:** block — trigger 3), DISTINCT from an explicit spike. On a source-touching diff this path still spawns the fresh-context adversarial judges over diff-derived criteria (provenance stays `adversarial-judged`); /flow:ship routes a source-touching no_plan_fallback to the draft manifest (declare criteria + re-verify, or human-waive). Absent/false ⇒ a plan governed this run.

visual_significant.description: The ONE authoritative visual-significance verdict (Feature 1a), computed by lib/visual-significance.py and stamped here so downstream steps (ship Step 5c distill + Step 7 dual-deliverable gate) read ONE value instead of re-deriving it (FB-0010 fan-out defense). True ⇒ this change touches a UI/asset surface (or a Visual-walk block / agent flag forced it) on a uiSurface!=false project, and is not a pure no-render-delta refactor. When true, the capture→buffer→render path (verify-build Steps 5a/8/10) is MANDATORY: a visually-significant change with ZERO captured frames is Unknown, never PASS. Absent/false ⇒ no visual deliverables are required by the ship gate. Additive in schema_version 1.0.

visual_signals.description: Human-readable evidence lines behind `visual_significant` (the WHY: which UI/asset files matched, whether an override fired, whether a refactor was excluded). Surfaced in the ship log + the skip-audit. Additive in schema_version 1.0.

overall_verdict.description: Aggregated verdict per Step 7. Any FAIL or any Unknown across dimensions/criteria aggregates here.

exit_code.description: 0 for PASS; 1 for FAIL or Unknown. Pins the gate-blocking contract.

criteria.description: One entry per criterion extracted from the plan (or per spike-rubric check in spike mode).

text.description: Verbatim criterion text from the plan's **Spec-walk:** block (or spike-rubric check name).

provenance.description: HOW this criterion's verdict was produced — the non-forgeability signal the Test-plan + report renderers key on. `adversarial-judged`: the Step-6 fresh-context judges ran (full mode, or the no-plan source-touching path). `spike-rubric`: an explicit-spike fresh-context correctness judge ran the 3-check rubric. `hand-authored`: the implementing agent wrote the verdict with NO fresh-context judge. LOAD-BEARING CONTRACT: this value is set by the JUDGING step, never the implementer — and a consumer MUST treat an ABSENT or unrecognized provenance as `hand-authored` (the untrusting default; an un-stamped buffer reads as un-judged / self-reported, never machine-judged). `adversarial-judged` and `spike-rubric` are 'machine-judged' (a fresh-context judge produced the verdict); `hand-authored`/absent is 'self-reported'. HONEST LIMITATION: this is a cooperative-agent contract, not a cryptographic one — the renderers trust a `adversarial-judged`/`spike-rubric` value verbatim and do not (yet) cross-verify it against judge evidence, so a non-cooperative author could write the trusted value. The mechanically ENFORCED guarantee is the inverse asymmetry: an absent/unrecognized stamp can NEVER produce a machine `[x]` (omission is defended; commission is roadmapped — 'second-source the provenance stamp'). Additive in schema_version 1.0; older consumers ignore it.

adversarial_cases.description: 0-2 adversarial cases per adversarial.md output. Empty array when criterion is fully covered by its text.

observations.description: Discrete observation captures from bundled /verify, classified by type. Empty array when criterion was not exercised.

type.description: Observation discriminator. 'narrative' captures freeform /verify prose; the others are structured captures the HTML renderer can lay out distinctly.

content.description: The captured content. For 'screenshot' this is a relative path or base64 data URI; for 'a11y_snapshot' it's the snapshot text; for 'network' it's the request/response summary; for 'console'/'log'/'stdout' it's the log line; for 'narrative' it's the prose excerpt.

timestamp_offset_ms.description: Optional milliseconds since verify start; helps HTML renderer order observations along a timeline.

aggregated_verdict.description: Per-criterion aggregation. PASS only if all three dimensions PASS. Any FAIL or Unknown propagates.

grounding.description: Optional. Why this surface looks/behaves as it does — the rationale column the HTML report renders (operationalizes FB-0040 'rationale carried' + FB-0037 designer-lens). Absent when no rationale was captured. Additive in schema_version 1.0; existing consumers ignore it.

type.description: Exactly one rationale type. 'need' = traced to a user job (cite it in citations); 'design-language' = follows a named system rule; 'craft-commitment' = a stated design stance; 'open-question' = no cohesive rationale yet (bridges to top-level open_questions). The anti-pattern is manufacturing a 'need' to launder a styling call.

citations.description: Citations resolved from config (specPath / designLanguagePath) or the plan's Spec-walk — never hardcoded project doc names.

decision_test.description: Optional — a named decision test run in-annotation, with its result.

not_tested.description: Closed-form per-platform checklist from lib/not-tested-checklist.md. Agent flips tested=true only where it actually exercised the surface.

tested.description: True only when the agent actually exercised this surface.

rationale.description: Optional one-sentence rationale, primarily for the false case.

open_questions.description: Optional. Subjective, human-facing questions surfaced for the merge gate — DISTINCT from an epistemic 'Unknown' verdict. 'Unknown' = 'I couldn't exercise this path'; an open_question = 'this is a taste/priority call only you should make'. Rendered as the report's standalone 'Open questions for you' block. A routing='this-iteration' entry blocks Step 8 auto-advance (mirrors an unresolved MEDIUM assumption); 'future-planning' routes to the roadmap. Additive in schema_version 1.0.

rationale.description: Our view + why (strongly recommended; the report renders it).

recommended_default.description: What keeps the loop moving if the human only half-glances (strongly recommended).

user_need_lens.description: Which job/persona the question is about.

routing.description: 'this-iteration' blocks Step 8 auto-advance until the human answers (a 'this is wrong' re-enters Execute); 'future-planning' routes to the roadmap, non-blocking.

evidence.description: Exactly two verbatim quotes per the two-citation rule. First quote is from /verify observations; second is from the criterion / adversarial case / plan rule.

notes.description: One-sentence rationale for FAIL or Unknown. Empty string for PASS.
